title: Concurrency, Cancellation, Timeouts, Retries, and Flake Control · Release Evidence Brief: Ci Concurrency Group Cancel Policy
summary: Two deployments race for one environment, an older run completes last, cancelled jobs skip cleanup, a hung process consumes the runner limit, and retries erase the first failure. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci concurrency group cancel policy increment in bounded execution and concurrency policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Build concurrency keys from workflow, branch, pull request, service, or environment identity and choose cancellation from business semantics.
Measure cold and warm dependency, build, test, upload, queue, and critical-path time while preserving a regular cold-path regression run.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.

  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2

  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example older deployment finishes after a newer revision, cancellation skips cleanup, a child process exceeds 5 minutes, and retries erase the first failure. Bound concurrency, staleness, timeouts, idempotency, and flake repair. Competency probe: Build concurrency keys from workflow, branch, pull request, service, or environment identity and choose cancellation from business semantics. A defensible solution applies Ci Concurrency Group Cancel Policy, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to bounded execution and concurrency policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-worked-gha-concurrency-timeouts-flake-control-4
  - competency: ci-worked-gha-concurrency-timeouts-flake-control-4
    artifact: execution-control.yaml
    decision: worked gha concurrency timeouts flake control 4
    environment: GitHub-hosted ubuntu-24.04 runner on a cold cache
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      concurrency-group: deploy-production-service-a
      cancel-in-progress: tests-only
      revision-guard: compare-before-mutate
      job-timeout-minutes: 20
      command-timeout-seconds: 90
      cleanup: always-idempotent
      retry: classified-transient-max-2
      first-failure: retained
      flake-owner-expiry: team-delivery-7-days
    verify: simulate overlapping revisions cancellation timeout transient failure duplicate side effect and cleanup interruption
    failure: Reject blanket cancellation, commit-order assumptions, timeout as cleanup, all-error retry, or ownerless quarantine.
    repair: Scope concurrency to shared state, reject stale mutation, bound every wait, preserve first failure, and expire quarantine.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: cancel-in-progress is always safe for tests and deployments. This fails the Ci Concurrency Group Cancel Policy contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
